headline: LSU's Offense Meets Oregon's Wounded Defense in Eugene
subhead: The nation's top team travels to face a Ducks squad searching for answers after a 34-16 loss to Penn State.

xFactor.why: Oregon gave up 34 points to Penn State and must tighten its secondary coverage to prevent LSU from establishing early rhythm downfield.

keyMatchup: LSU's passing attack, which carved Miami for 38 points, versus an Oregon secondary that surrendered 16 points in the first half alone to the Nittany Lions.
body: LSU arrives undefeated and ranked first after dispatching Miami in a shootout that showed both offensive firepower and vulnerability to the deep ball. The Tigers will have every reason to test Oregon's corners early and often, especially after the Ducks allowed Penn State to establish control through the air. Oregon's Week 1 collapse was less about effort and more about execution—dropped passes, coverage busts, and an inability to pressure the opposing quarterback—all correctable problems that Bo Nix and the offense can exploit if given time.

The real question is whether Oregon's defense can force LSU into mistakes or whether the Ducks offense can keep pace if they fall behind. Nix showed poise against Penn State despite limited protection, but LSU's front will present a stiffer challenge. If Oregon trails by two scores heading into the fourth quarter, the momentum of a ranked road team with championship pedigree becomes nearly impossible to overcome.
